content.problemStatement:
You are given a matrix BBB of size N×KN \times KN×K, i.e. NNN rows and KKK columns. B(i,j)B_{(i, j)}B(i,j)​ denotes the value in the cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j). You can buy the iii-th row of the matrix for AiA_iAi​ coins.

Your goal is to buy a non-empty subset of the rows, and then create a set with KKK cells such that the set has exactly one cell from each column; but you are restricted to pick cells only from the rows that you have bought.

The beauty of the chosen set of cells is the sum of the values of the cells. Let XXX denote the beauty, and CCC denote the total cost of the rows that you bought; maximize (X−C)(X - C)(X−C) and print the maximum value.


Input Format

The first line contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK.
The iii-th of the next NNN lines each contain K+1K + 1K+1 integers - Ai,B(i,1),…,B(i,K)A_i, B_{(i, 1)}, \ldots, B_{(i, K)}Ai​,B(i,1)​,…,B(i,K)​. Here, B(i,j)B_{(i, j)}B(i,j)​ denotes the value on the cell in the iii-th row and jjj-th column.


Output Format
Output the maximum value of X−CX - CX−C.
constraints: **Subtask 3 (11 points):** Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0 for all 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There is only one row, and we must buy it. Then, our set will have beauty 2+7+1=102 + 7 + 1 = 102+7+1=10 and for a cost of 555. Hence, the answer is 10−5=510 - 5 = 510−5=5.

Explanation:
The optimal solution is to buy the second and third rows, and then choose the cells B(2,2)B_{(2, 2)}B(2,2)​, B(3,1)B_{(3, 1)}B(3,1)​, B(3,3)B_{(3, 3)}B(3,3)​ to form the set, where B(x,y)B_{(x, y)}B(x,y)​ denotes the value of the cell on the xxx-th row and yyy-th column.
This has a beauty of 101010 and a cost of 333.
